name: TOI00749.01
comments: EPHEMERIS ERROR: 8 min Submitted to TOP 3/2/2020 Report: Signal is on-target and detected in all four bands (griz). Strong chromaticity is detected, indicating an EB.
comments_sg1: Likely EB; HATSouth has extensive follow-up of this candidate, and suggests that it's actually double the period with a nearly equal secondary eclipse. RVs show no variation, suggesting a blend or grazing SB2 with blended lines. From a follow-up light curve on the odd phase at 2xP, there is apparent eccentricity due to the flux minimum being centered at phase 0.49. Joe Twicken analyzed the SPOC TESS light curve and found a phase shift limit in odd/even epochs that differs at the 2.26 sigma level, which is ~10 times smaller than suggested by the HATS follow-up. Will Waalkes observed a full ODD transit on 20190614 in Ic and confirmed the 50 ppt event ~5 minutes early and on target using a 4 aperture that is contaminated with a 1.5" neighbor that, considering the Tmag uncertainty, may be bright enough to have caused the TESS detection. Felipe and the MuSCAT2 team observed a full on 2020124 in gp,rp,ip,zs and detected a chromatic transit on target."

name: TOI03896.01
comments: Observed on 20 December 2023, Report: We observed full on 20231220 in gp, rp, ip, zs and did not detect an event using 10.875 aperture. We excluded the data after 2460299.73 BJD due to the high humidity. We did not use ID3 as a comparison star because its position overlapped with that of ID0 (target star). We can rule out all the identified nearby stars. There are several faint sources that are not identified by the pipeline. Observed on May 11. Report: We tentatively detect a transit event on target. We can rule out 1 star as EB with deep transit during the window The lack of pre-transit observations, gap in the data, and the g-band light curve (with deeper transit than the others) makes me a little wary to claim a detection. Let's get a second transit"
comments_sg1: found in faint-star QLP search; Ferran Grau/CALOU-0m4 analyzed a full on 20230315 in r and possibly detected a 14 min (2.9σ) early 1.7 ppt full (with time and MF detrending) using an uncontaminated 4.2 target aperture. SPOC is consistent with on-target, and these data clear the nearest star T2 (18" away). Felipe Murgas/MuSCAT2 observed a slightly defocused egress on 20230511 in gp,rp,ip,zs and tentatively detected a ~15 min. early ~3.8, 1.0, 0.6, 2.0 ppt event using an uncontaminated 10.9" target aperture. JeromeL/ MUSCAT2 Team/ observed full on 2023.12.20 in gp, rp, ip, zs and probably detected a hint of a ~2 ppt event in the combined light curve using uncontaminated 10.9" target apertures. The gp band light curve shows an apparent ~on-time event, although the RMS is still high. Inconcusive. The data are just not sensitve enough at the ~ 1ppt level. Howie Relles/ KarenC/ LCO-McD-1m0 analyzed a full on 20240404 in ip and detected an ~on-time 1.5 ppt event using an uncontaminated 5.8" target aperture. No more observations needed, except for extreme precision (0.5 ppt/10 min) **full-only**, **multi-band-only** data in at least blue (B, g') *and* mid (V, rp) *and* red (i', I, z, Y) filters to refine the check on chromaticity."

name: TOI01835.01
comments: Rafa: Multi planet. Very interested planet to be proposed for CARMENES Period may be different, the transit at TJD 1932.8 and 1938.4 appears to be similar in depth and duration, the transit at TJD 1944.0 is coincident with a momentum dump, and can't be recovered from the data. There are three more possible single events near 1937.9, 1947.5 and 1949.0. The 1949.0 event (in report for candidate .02) has the largest depth. Observed on 04/03/2022. Report: The i-band CCD had connection issues and could not be used. We used Star ID 1 as reference since Star ID 2 had issues with its photometry. We have a tentative transit detection on target. The blend analysis was done with Star ID 2 since is the only star outside TESS aperture but due to the photometry issues is useless. Observed on 07/06. Report: The observations were affected by clouds, CCD1 presented issues according to the End of night report. The transit could not be fitted and there is a chance we missed the transit according to the ephemeris; it could be that the observations were planned with an old ephemeris. Data are useless.

name: TOI05172.01
comments: Observed on Jan 5, 2025. Report: We observed a full on 20250105 in gp, rp, ip, zs and did not detect an event using an 10.9 uncontaminated target aperture. We can rule out deep eclipses in all the identified nearby stars."
comments_sg1: [P=4.7218077] Rick Schwarz/LCO-CTIO-1m0 analyzed a full on 20220410 in zs and probably detected a 77 min (1.4σ) early 1.4 ppt event in an uncontaminated 4.3 target aperture and cleared 12/12 neighbors. [P=4.7198291] Roberto Zambelli /KarenC /LCO-SAAO-1m0 analyzed a full on 20220517 in zs and probably ruled out a 1.4 ppt event in an uncontaminated 5.8" target aperture. There is a possible shallower 0.8 ppt V-shaped event, but the depth depends on comp ensemble. Taking an independent look at the previous light curve from 20220410, I'm not convinced that the reported early detection is astrophysical. Its depth is highly dependent on comp ensemble, and is shallowest using the two best comp star, so I think is likely a systematic. There are no more TESS observations scheduled at this time, so let's try 2σ late. [P=4.7247631] SPOC centroids limit event to on-target. Rick Schwarz/LCO-SSO-1m0 analyzed a nominal full on 20230302 in zs and possibly detected a 190 min (net 2.9σ) late 0.9 ppt ingress using an uncontaminated 5.1" target aperture. [P=4.7261363] HowieR/ KarenC/ LCO-TEID-1m0 analyzed a full with high sky background on 20230306 in zs. The observations are inconclusive due to strong trends relative to the expected 1.4 ppt depth, but in a trimmed version with time detrending, there is a possible ~on-time 1.2 ppt event on-target, but using all data and airmass+time, the event is not seen.Jerome de Leon/ MuSCAT2 team/ observed a full on 20250105 in gp, rp, ip, zs and did not firmly detect a ~1.4 ppt event using an uncontaminated 10.9" target aperture. We can rule out deep eclipses in all the identified nearby stars. There appears to be a hint of a ~60 min late <1 ppt event in the combined light curve, however, the data are not precise at the ~1 ppt level to confirm. The ephemeris uncertiantly is quite large. There is another TESS visit April 2025. For now, let's return to nominal. [P=4.7218077] Update from AIJ-s91. [P=4.7268977]. Confirm event on target and/or check field for NEBs within 2.5' using a red (rp, R, ip, I, z) filter."

name: TOI06884.01
comments: observed on 3rd of May 2024 Report: We detect a transit event on the target star.
comments_sg1: [P=14.4217699] Period is 1/3 according to TRES RVs. additional event at TBJD 2655.5; possible eccentric EB; CTOI from Kendra Nguyen (SPOC-CTOI); Bob Massey/ CristilynW/ LCO-TEID analyzed a weather-affected nominal full (e-175/i+361 min;-0.5σ/+1.1σ) on 20240211 in ip. Inconclusive due to large hit to the sky during the predicted event. HowieR/ CristilynW/ LCO-HAL-0m35 analyzed a nominal full (e-410 /i+230 min; -1.2/+0.7σ ) on UT20240321 in ip and ruled out the ~2.3 ppt event using an uncontaminated 6.6 target aperture. There are no upcoming TESS observations. Let's try 1.5σ late. [P=4.8096089]. KarenC / LCO-McD-1m0 analyzed a full at the 1.5σ late ephem on 20240405 in ip and likely detected a 320 min early (net 0.1σ late) 2.3 ppt egress at very high airmass. However, airmass was very high at the end of the lightcurve too and no similar dip was seen. A previous weather-affected observation from 20240211 was consistent with a 2 ppt event at the ephemeris implied above. An previous observation from 20240221 had a tiny bit of coverage of ingress at the ephemeris implied above, but there is no hint of a 2.3 ppt ingress if the timing is precise. [P=4.8082046, net 0.1σ late] Allyson Bieryla/ KeplerCam observed an egress+20% on 20240405 in ip and possibly detected a 25 min (0.07σ) late ~2 ppt egress using an uncontaminated 5.4" target aperture. KarenC/ LCO-Hal-MusCAT3 observed an intended full on 20240419 in gp,rp,ip,zs and ruled out a 2.4 ppt event on-target in the nominal pre-ingress baseline. The observation was cut short at nominal ingress due to an LCO telescope command failure. The data were also weather affected, requiring double detrending to flatten the baseline. Rick Schwarz/LCO-McD-1m0 analyzed a full on 20240424 in ip and detected an on-time ~2.1 ppt event using an uncontaminated 4.3" target aperture. [uP=0.001] Micaela Magno/ Francis Wilkin/ LCO-McD-1m0 analyzed an egress+80% on 20240429 in ip and detected a ~on-time ~2.5 ppt [(Rp/R*)²: 2.2 ppt] egress using an uncontaminated 7.0" target aperture and cleared 3/3 neighbors for NEBs over the window. This observation re-confirms the revised ephemeris. Felipe Murgas/ MuSCAT2 observed a full on 20240503 in gp, rp, ip, zs and detected a ~6 min. early (Rp/R*)²: 2.8, 2.1, 2.5, 1.2 ppt ] event using an uncontaminated 10.9" target aperture. Rick Schwarz/ Khalid Barkaoui/ LCO-HAL-M3/ analyzed a nominal full (turned out to be egress+90%) on 20241225 in gp-rp-ip-zs and detected a ~15 min (0.5σ) late ~2.6 ppt (average
depth_sg1: (Rp/R*)²: 2.0, 2.0, 2.4, & 2.4 ppt) egress using uncontaminated 6.9" to 7.2" target apertures. [P=4.8082535] The next observation should be a high precision (<1.0 ppt/10 min) full transit in a red (r', R, i', I, z) filter to check for the return of the transit at the revised ephemeris."

name: TOI01823.01
comments_sg1: [P=194.0674433] Two transits separated by 194d. Possible periods: 194d 97d 64.7d 48.5 or 38.8. Kim McLeod analyzed a (unintentional) heavily defocused egress (e-281/+27min; ephem sig=1 min, but duration sig=19min) from 20210118 in LCO-Teid-0m4 zs that either rules out an on-time event on target, or detects a 20 min early ~9 ppt egress and clears the field to 2.5' at the 65d period in an uncontaminated 8 aperture. Felipe and the MuSCAT2 team observed an egress -300/+30 min (Tc σ=1 min, dur σ=19 min) on 20210117 in gp,rp,ip,zs and ruled out the event over the observed window. Although only ~1.5σ of duration uncertainty was covered on the late side, egress duration is ~30 minutes (from SPOC), so the start of an egress would have likely been seen, if at this period, given the total of 30 min coverage + 30 minute duration uncertainty (~3σ coverage). Jana Steuer and the Mount Wendelstein team observed a near full on 20210212 in gp, ip and detected roughly on-time ~9, 9 ppt events on target. This is the 14th transit since T0 assuming the 39 day period. The 39 day period is 192 days/5, so this is the 4th of 5 orbits since the last 192 day super period transit. Hopefully someone else will more carefully check, but I think this data uniquely constrain the period to ~39 days. Chris Mann & the NEOSSat Team observed a gapped full on 20210220 in clear and detected a 9 ppt transit on target, reconfirming the 39 day period. [P=38.8134887] Chris Mann and the NEOSSat team observed a full on 20210323 and detected a ~10 ppt transit in an uncontaminated 10.5" target aperture. Paul Dalba confirms this is consistent with APF RVs. Chris Mann observed full transit -187/+120 min on 20210825 with a clear filter using NEOSSat and detected an on-time, 12 ppt transit. Chris Mann observed an ingress on 20211220 gp,rp and detected an on-time 9.6 ppt ingress using variable aperture radii to track changing FWHM. AdamP/SUTO2-0m3 observed a full on 20220415 in R and and with MF+Airmass detrending possibly detected a roughly on-time ~7 ppt transit. Ellie Heldridge/Thacher-CDK700 observed a full on 20230331 in rp and detected an ~on-time ~10 ppt event using an uncontaminated 7.3" target aperture. K. Barkaoui/ TRAPPIST-N-0.6m observed an egress+60% on 20231227 in B and detected an ~8 min early ~9 ppt event using an uncontaminated 4.2” target aperture. K. Barkaoui/ TRAPPIST-N-0.6m observed an ingress+50% on 20240708 in zp and detected an ~on-time ~8 ppt ingress using an uncontaminated 6.5” target aperture. K. Barkaoui/ LCO-Teid-0m4/ analyzed an egress+50% on 20250226 in ip and detected an ~on-time ~7.6 ppt egress using an uncontaminated 3.1” target aperture. No more SG1 observations needed."

name: TOI05626.01
comments_sg1: [P=716.2249064] duo transit; likely P>22d; Revised T0 from Gaia Lacedelli <gaia.lacedelli@iac.es> for 2025-02-27. Don't know the period. Greg Srdoc/Kotizarovci-0.3m observed a defocused egress +50% in-transit on 20250227 in ~TESS-band and ruled out a 9 ppt egress over a -82/+425 min window relative to nominal egress using an uncontaminated 12.0 target aperture. Update from AIJ-s23-s49-s76 and T0 from s23 [P=716.2249064, =1943.807067871]. Update from Gaia Lacedelli to likely Period [P=23.1037430, T0=3745.9159] ChrisS/ LCO-HAL-0m35/ analyzed an intended full (cut short to ingress+80%) on 20250403 in ip and detected an ~on-time ~10.8 ppt [(Rp/R*)²: 11.6 ppt] ingress using an uncontaminated 5.1" target aperture and cleared the neighbors over the window. Rick Schwarz/ Khalid Barkaoui/ LCO Key Project/ LCO-HAL-M3 analyzed a defocused intended full cut short by weather (i+64%) on 20250403 in MUSCAT gp-rp-ip-zs and detected a ~6 min (6.0σ) late (assuming the 2.89 hr duration) ~9 ppt achromatic ingress using 7" target apertures. (Rp/R*)²: 9.8 ppt (gp), 9.2 ppt (rp), 9.2 ppt (ip) & 10.0 ppt (zs). The next observation should be a high precision (<1.0 ppt/10 min) full-only transit in a red (r', R, i', I, z) filter to check for the return of the transit at the revised ephemeris. High-precision (<1.0 ppt/10 min) multi-band or blue (U, u', B, g') filter observations even better for a simultaneous check on chromaticity."

name: TOI01268.01
comments: EPHEMERIS ERROR: 12 min Observed on 24 Feb. 2023: Report: We detect a deep transit event on target. I guess this is not for TFOP since it is a discovered planet (TOI-1268b).
comments_sg1: We now have 5 TRES observations. It's looking like the semi-amplitude will be challenging for TRES. Would imply a companion of about the mass of Jupiter. Greg Srdoc observed an ingress + ~30% on 20200109 in ~TESS-band and confirmed an on-time 7-10 ppt ingress on target using a 10.8 aperture that is only marginally contaminated with 18-20" (possibly extended) neighbor that was not detected by Gaia. Carl Z. observed an egress + 25% on 20200110 in 0m4 zs and confirmed an 8-10 ppt on-time egress in the 10" uncontaminated target aperture. The observations were intended to be focused but were significantly defocused due to a robot error. Carl Ziegler observed a full on 2020126 in DSW gp and confirmed a 10 ppt on-time transit in a 10.9" target aperture. Khalid and the TRAPPIST-N team observed a near-full on 20200306 in B and possibly detected a roughly 7.5 ppt event that is complicated by a meridian flip at ingress and a strong increase in sky background as the nominal time of egress approached. Pablo Lewin observed fulls on 20200203 in 0m4 gp and zs. Transits with depth consistent with TESS were detected in both filters. Raquel and Jose observed an ingress +90% on 20210418 in OAUV-TURIA1 B-band. There's a possible ingress, but the interpretation is complicated by a MF during predicted ingress and strong residuals. Ferran Grau observed an nominal full on 20210418 in CALOU-0m4 and detected a near-full roughly 10 ppt transit on target. Kim McLeod and students observed an ingress (i-17/+106 min
depth_sg1: i-8.5/+53 σ) on 20210427 in g in an uncontaminated 4.7" aperture for a chromaticity check and measured a 11ppt ingress that is possibly ~10min late. No more SG1 observations needed. Published."

In an alternate reduction, I see a ~50 minute (~1.5-sigma) early ~2.7 ppt ingress in an 4" target aperture, but the light curve has a slightly lower BIC with simple airmass detrending and no transit model included. The nearest neighbor also showed an apparent V-shaped event, but the shape is inconsistent with the SPOC transit shape. Felipe and the MuSCAT2 team observed a full on 20200228 in gp,rp,ip,zs and detected an ~on-time achromatic (except rp) ~2.7 ppt transit in a marginally contaminated 6.5" target aperture. PereG observed a full on 20200521 in OAA Ic and likely detected a ~2ppt arriving on time in a 8.6" contaminated target aperture. Akihiko and the MuSCAT team observed fulls on 20210408 in gp,rp,zs and detected on-time ~achromatic ~3 ppt transits. Confirmed planet."

name: TOI05599.01
comments: Observed on 14th of February 2024 Report: The observations were affected by clouds. We have poor coverage of before and after the transit. The transit event is too shallow to be detected given the scatter in the light curves. I think the data are useless. This is a difficult target due to the lack of good reference stars I would try another observation if possible moving the FoV. An option would be to put the target star in the lower left corner to try to get another bright reference star observed on 3rd of April 2024 Report: The transit is not detected with significance because the reference stars are too faint. The data are useless. To the observers: when you see that the target star has no good reference stars if the field is centered it is ok to change the FoV and put the target star off center to find better references. For this case by moving the target to the left and little bit down a bright reference star could be place inside the field (this was a note in the comments).

comments_sg1: [P=2.09098] Slight depth-aperture correlation; John Kielkopf observed a full on 20200525 in ULMT rp and detected a 10 min (1 sigma) late 20 ppt transit in a 3.2 uncontaminated target aperture. [P=2.091205] CarlZ observed a full on 20200527 in rp and detected a roughly 15 ppt transit. Ryan Cloutier analyzed an intended full on 20200531 in ip likely detected a ~10 min late ~15 ppt ingress on target. CarlZ observed a full on 20200525 in rp and detected a roughly 30 ppt transit on target. Felipe and the MuSCAT2 team observed an egress+50% on 20200608 in gp,rp,ip,zs and detected a likely achromatic ~25 ppt transit. PereG observed a full on 20210315 in OAA Ic and detected an on-time ~20 ppt V-shaped event in a 8.6" uncontaminated target aperture. Raquel and Jose observed a full on 20210218 in OAUV-T50 V and detected a 15-20 ppt V-shaped event. Eric Jensen further analyzed with a joint MCMC fit and finds that although the depths (flux deficit at mid-transit) are monotonically increasing from blue to red, the Rp values are consistent across bands with no dilution (i.e. limb darkening differences across bands account for the depth differences). [P=2.0912042] Felipe and the MuSCAT2 team observed fulls on 20210521 in rp,ip,zs and detected ~on-time ~19 ppt transits in all bands on target. No more observations needed at this time. Gaia finds possible confirmation on target. Awaiting RV or validation interest."

name: TOI03908.01
comments: EPHEMERIS ERROR: 12min USP Report: The i-band CCD had connection issues and could not be used. We detect a deep chromatic signal on target. The eclipse seems slightly delayed with a longer duration than the TESS detection. By eye, the eclipse should actually be deeper in the z-band than what the model shows. Also, the short pre-ingress baseline might have affected the results. Send to TFOP and moved to priority 5
comments_sg1: V-shaped; found in faint-star QLP search; The velocities from the four TRES observations fit a linear drift down rather well. There is a stellar companion in this system that is not responsible for the transit events. Felipe Murgas/MuSCAT2 observed a full on 20220410 in gp,rp,zs and detected a ~8 min late 5.67, 11.57, 14.92 ppt event using an uncontaminated 10.87 target aperture. There are no AO companions reported and the MuSCAT2 show the event on-target relative to Gaia DR3 stars. SPOC s14-s50 reports Rp = 25 +/- 8 Re and the event is V-shaped. Gaia RUWE is a little high at 2.76. Maybe the binary in a hierarchical triple is causing the V-shape eclipse and the RV drift? If anyone is interested in further follow-up on a complex system, let me know. Retired as APC/BEB/RVD."

Felipe and the MuSCAT2 team observed a full on 20201221 in gp,rp,ip,zs and tentatively detected an ~on-time ~ 1 ppt transit in ip, zs (but not in gp, rp) in a 7.4" target aperture that is contaminated with a dT=3.5 neighbor 2.9" N. This could be hinting that the event is in the dT=3.5 neighbor 2.9" N. The MuSCAT team observed a full on 20210310 in gp,rp,zs and an apparently detected a chromatic transit where gp and rp are the correct depth, but zs is 3-4x too deep. The zp depths are quite different between the two MuSCATx measurements. Peyton Brown and KarenC observed fulls on 20210531 in rp,ip,zs (gp was saturated) and ruled out an on-time 1.2 ppt event on target, and detected a ~40 ppt V-shaped NEB in the 3" neighbor T2 (TIC 1001039596, T = 13.3, ΔT = 3.5, r = 2.9" N), which is about the expected depth in the neighbor. The SPOC light curve is also v-shaped. Retired as NEB."

While our photometry for the target star would be sufficient to detect the event securely, the available comparison stars are faint and introduce a significant amount of noise to the relative photometry. Thus, we cannot securely detect the event on the target from the relative photometry.
comments_sg1: Centroid offset at 3 sigma; but consistent with a bright star.; Justin Wittrock observed a gapped full on 20200514 in R. T4 and T5 were cleared. name: TOI03899.01
comments: Observed on 21/04. Report: The i-band CCD had connection issues and could not be used. We do not detect the transit event on target. We can only ruled out as EBs 2 stars during the observed window. The pipeline fits an egress at the beginning of the observations but I think it is not real, there is some X-axis oscilations that kind of end at the fitted egress so I'm skeptical of the fit. Let's try a second observation Observed on 15 April. Report: We restricted the aperture size to avoid flux contamination by a nearby star. The i-band light curve was more affected by systematics. We do not detect the transit event with significance; there is a hint of a transit in the 10 min. binned light curve of a transit fit done using g-, r-, zs- bands. We can rule out 2 stars as EBs with deep transits in the observed window.

Let's check 3.7σ early. [P=5.2069856] Felipe Murgas/MuSCAT2 observed a nominal full on 20230415 in gp,rp,ip,zs and tentatively detected a ~24 min late 0.67, 0.73, 2.28 (very noisy undetrended), 0.57 ppt event using an uncontaminated 6.96 target aperture. [P=5.2070669] Howie Relles/LCO-TEID-1m0 analyzed a nominal full cut short on 20230506 in ip and possibly detected a ~29 min. late ~1 ppt ingress+90% in an uncontaminated 8.2" target aperture.[P=5.2070793]. [P=5.598738] Howie Relles/LCO-CTIO-1m0 analyzed a nominal ingress on 20220703 in zs and probably detected a ~15 min (0.1σ) early 2.63 ppt ingress in an uncontaminated 5.4" target aperture. [P=5.598666] The next observation should be a high precision (<1.0 ppt/5 min) full transit in a red (r', R, i', I, z, Y) filter to attempt a firm detection of the event on target. Multi-band or blue (U, u', B, g') filter observations preferred for a simultaneous chromaticity check."

name: TOI01830.01
comments: EPHEMERIS ERROR: 1 min Retired by SG1
comments_sg1: large planet; variable star; Marcus Rabus reports NRES RVs indicating an eclipsing binary (>20 km/s) on a very eccentric orbit. John Kielkopf observed a defocused full on 20200516 in ULMT zp and detected an on-time ~19 ppt transit in an 8.2 uncontaminated aperture. It kind of looks like there's an overlapping ~2 ppt transit that ingresses about mid-transit. There are hints of another event in the SPOC report, but maybe it's just variability?? Retired as SEB1."

name: TOI03984.01
comments: This object was introduced as a validation target for M2. Planet around M star. WE have a beautiful transit, ket get a few more for a calidation paper April 2023: A mass determination paper for TOI3984 has already appeared on arXiv. https://ui.adsabs.harvard.edu/abs/2023arXiv230207714C/abstract Abandoned
comments_sg1: [P=4.3529160] found in faint-star QLP search; Tianjun Gan reports that 6 NEID observations spaced around the orbtial phase ruled out an orbit at < 5 m/s at this period. K. Barkaoui/LCO-Teid-1m0 analyzed a full on 20220414 in ip and detected a ~100 min (2σ) late 22 ppt event on the target using an uncontaminated 4.7 target aperture (although it is contaminated with TIC 1101522311, T = 18.7, ΔT = 5.2, r = 3.3" NNW, which is nominally too faint to be responsible for the detection). KarenC reduced with an uncontaminated 2.3" target aperture, excluding flux from the too faint neighbor, and confidently confirmed the event on target. [P=4.353343] K. Barkaoui/Artemis-1m0 observed a full on 20220427 in I+z and detected an ~on time ~23ppt event on the target using an uncontaminated 3.8" target aperture. K. Barkaoui, F.J. Pozuelos/OSN-1.5m observed an ingress on 20220510 in V and Ic filters and detected on-time ~22 ppt ingresses using an uncontaminated 4.5" target aperture. The transit looks achromatic. K. Barkaoui/LCO-Teid-1m0 analyzed a full on 20220606 in gp and detected ~on-time 29 ppt (slightly larger than other filters, dF_ip = 23.5ppt and dF_JI = 22.7ppt) event on the target using an uncontaminated 2.3" target aperture. Rp/R* is slightly larger too, but I'm not sure the baselines are good enough across the datasets to make a firm chromaticity ruling yet. No more observations needed, except for **full-only** multi-band or alternating filter high precision (<1.0 ppt/5 min) data in at least blue (U, u', B, g') *and* red (i', I, z, Y) filters to refine the check on chromaticity."

name: TOI01860.01
comments: Akihiko: added to fill in the night of June 4, 2020. Transit is too shallow (0.23 ppt) and there is no good comparison star, so only to clear the field. Submitted to TFOP 29/7/2020 Report: We let the target star saturate to clear the field. The transit signal is not visible on any of the nearby stars and we can reliably clear all the stars except the one that is too close to the target for reliable photometry (ID 5).
comments_sg1: [P=1.0662322] 2 other stars in pixel (TIC 202426246 and 1102367690 are the same star and one is a phantom); possible EB; David Baker and students observed a full on 20200606 in Ic and cleared 4 of 8 nearby stars. The 5 neighbor T2 is not cleared. Felipe and the MuSCAT2 team observed a deep full on 20200720 in gp,rp,ip,zs and cleared the field of NEBs, except for the dT = 5.8 neighbor 5" E. Bob Massey analyzed a deep full (e-164/i+161 min
depth_sg1: +/-6σ) on 20210411 in 1m0 zs. There's a hint at a 90 min early NEB in T2 (TIC 1102367690, dT=5.83, r=5.5"), but its far form conclusive. Rick Schwarz/Karen/LCO-Teid-1m0 analyzed a full on 20220628 in zs and possibly detected an ~18 min (2σ) early ~50 ppt event using 1.2" apertures in star T2 (TIC 1102367690, T = 13.6, ΔT = 5.8, r = 5.5" W), which PHARO AO imaging reports as a binary. This planet has been validated, and these results should not be considered conclusive given the significant blending in the T2 aperture by the 5.8 mag brighter target that is 5.5" away. So, let's try again. [P=1.0662195] Bob Massey/LCO-TEID analyzed a full on 20230301 in zs and (despite non-photometric skies) using opposite aperture detrending, probably ruled an NEB in T2 (TIC 1102367690 T = 13.616 ΔT = 5.83 r = 5.51"). The other neighbors were previous cleared by SPOC and SG1, but a potential NEB signal had been seen in T2. No more SG1 observations needed."

name: TOI02268.01
comments: observed on 1 February 2024 The observations were made with the telescope defocused. We do not detect the transit event with significance (transit is shallow), there could be a hint of a transit in the 10 min. binned light curve. We can rule out 1 star as an EB with deep transits.
comments_sg1: [P=7.156602] Georgia Mraz & KarenC analyzed a full (e-127/i+186 min; -1.9/+2.7σ) on 20210331 in 1m0 zs and possibly ruled out the 0.7 ppt event on target using a 5.8 aperture and airmass detrending. Howie Relles/LCO-McD-1m0 analyzed a nominal full (-1/+0.5σ) on 20230223 in ip under somewhat rough skies but did not see a convincing 0.7 ppt event. SPOC limits the event to on-target. AIJ analysis of s51 FFIs shows the event happened 270 min (1.5σ) late and after these observations ended. [P=7.1578520] Felipe Murgas/ MuSCAT2 observed a defocused full on 20240201 in gp,rp,ip,zs and cleared 1 out of 5 neighbors over the window. ChrisS/LCO-Teid-1m0 analyzed a cloud-impacted full on 20240421 in ip. Inconclusive
depth_sg1: Not sensitive at 0.5 ppt. Rick Schwarz/ LCO-McD-1m0/ analyzed a defocused (2.0) full on 20250208 in ip and probably detected an ~25 min (0.7σ) early ~0.7 ppt [(Rp/R*)²: 0.7 ppt] event using an uncontaminated 8.2" target aperture. [P=7.157781] Update to AIJ s77 [P=7.157421] RobertoZ/ KarenC /LCO-TEID-1m0 analyzed a nominal full on 20250420 in ip and ruled out the ~0.6 ppt event over the window. TEV does not record any new SPOC detections since sector 24. Looking at individual sectors 23, 24, 50, 51, and 77 with AIJ, there are apparent events in all sectors except 50, but they appear to be moving around in phase, suggesting long super-period TTVs. Could TTVs be washing out the signal in the SPOC pipeline?? Using T0 from sector 23 and the best fit period from s77, the revised AIJ period is [P=7.1574210] days, suggesting that the event should have ended just as these observations started. However it has been another year since the last TESS observation, so any TTVs make the expected ephemris uncertain this year. ChrisS/ KarenC/ LCO-Teid-1m0 analyzed a defocused full (-170/+170 min
plan_observations: -4.5σ/+4.5σ) on 20250427 in ip and likely ruled out an on-time 0.7 ppt event, but apparently detected a ~73 min early (2σ), 15 min (0.5σ) shorter, 0.7 ppt event using an uncontaminated 11" target aperture. No more observations needed, except for extreme precision (<0.3 ppt/10 min) full transits to detect the shallow event on target in a red (r', R, i', I, z, Y) filter."

Rick Schwarz/LCO-Teid-1m0 analyzed a full on 20230612 in ip and detected a ~29.0 min late ~ 2.0 ppt [(Rp/R*)²: 1.69 ppt] event using a 6.2" target aperture.Photometric results are inconsistent across g->z. [P=0.3576647] Update from AIJ-s15+s50. [P=0.3576596] No more observations needed, except for **full-only** multi-band or alternating filter high precision (<1.0 ppt/10 min) data in at least blue (U, u', B, g') *and* red (i', I, z, Y) filters to refine the check on chromaticity at the revised ephemeris."

name: TOI03922.01
comments: Observed on April 23, 2023 Report: The target star is blended with a nearby star (Star ID 1, TIC 1101855080). We removed g-band from the transit fit due to its large scatter, probably caused by centroid issues. We detect an achromatic transit in r-, i-, zs-bands; we can't establish if the transit occurs on the target star or the nearby star.
comments_sg1: [P=3.8959760] Same signal as TOI-3983.01, which is reportedly a phantom, so it has been retired as an FA. V-shaped; found in faint-star QLP search; Hunter Harling observed a nominal ingress on event 20220219 in Rc and detected a ~15 minute late (0.7σ) >10 ppt ingress using an 8.1 target aperture that is contaminated with TIC 1101855080 (T = 11.8 ΔT = -0.4 r = 2.1" SW). [P=3.896021] KarenC/ LCO-Hal-0m35 analyzed an egress (ingress weathered out) on 20230120 in ip and detected an ~on-time ~8 ppt egress (with two data points pre-ingress baseline) using an 8.4" target aperture that is contaminated with TIC 1101855080 (T = 11.8, ΔT = -0.4, r = 2.1" SW). Centroid shift analysis was inconclusive due to the weather gap in the data, and attempts to separate the two stars were inconclusive. Felipe Murgas/MuSCAT2 observed a defocused full on 20230423 in rp,ip,zs and detected an on-time ~5.3, 7.2, 7.2 ppt event [(Rp/R*)²: 0.0792, 0.0841, 0.0842 ppm] using a contaminated 6.09" target aperture. Ferran Grau/ CALOU-0m4/ analyzed a full on 20240722 in rp and detected an ~on-time, ~10 min longer duration, ~10.5 ppt event using a contaminated 6.2" target aperture (T2: dT=-0.4, r=2.1" SW). No more observations needed, except a focused full transit in a red (r', R, i', I, z) filter to separate the 2.1" SW neighbor and determine the source of the event."

name: TOI05687.01
comments: Observed on May 20. 2023- Report: Due to some configuration issues the zs-band data acquisition started later than the other bands. We detect a V-shaped and chromatic transit on target.
comments_sg1: [P=2.9244390] found in faint-star QLP search; Allyson Bieryla/KeplerCam observed a ingress (e-192/i+70 min: e-3.8σ/i+1.4σ) on 20230210 in ip and ruled out the 11.1 ppt event on target with an uncontaminated aperture and cleared 8/8 neighbors over the window. No more TESS observations currently planned. Let's try 3σ late. [P=2.925617] Don Radford observed a full transit on 20230503 in R and detected a ~10 min late 8 ppt event on target using uncontaminated 6.4 variable aperture. Paul Benni/ASP observed a full on 20230518 in gp and detected a 7 min (0.1σ) late 3.2 ppt (lower than predicted 11 ppt, suggests chromatic transit depths) event using uncontaminated 6.0" target apertures. Felipe Murgas/MuSCAT2 observed a nominal full on 20230520 in gp rp, ip, zs and detected an ~on-time 2.7, 7.1, 10.0, 12.2 ppt event using an uncontaminated 10.9" target aperture. No more SG1 observations needed."

name: TOI05268.01
comments: M&Ms target Tianjun Gan Program: Giant planet candidate around an M star, it is important to get good S/N in g and r bands. EPHEMERIS ERROR: 54min Observed on 24/03. Report: The i-band CCD had connection issues and could not be used. We detect a deep, V-shaped transit in r- and zs-bands. The scatter in g-band is too large to detect signs of the transit with significance (star is faint in this band, poor S/N). The host star is an M-dwarf (Teff = 3162 K). We should decide if we upload this to TFOP, although it seems that this is likely an EB. Observed on May 16 2024 Report: We detect a transit event on target. Checking the color dependency of the transit depth is hard because the target is faint in blue bands (B = 19.4 mag); the transit looks grazing and the g-band transit depth is consistent with i- and zs-bands considering the large uncertainty.
comments_sg1: [P=2.0685536] found in faint-star QLP search; Aviad, Tsevi, Shay, and Avraham report from 55 Gaia data points (2 in-transit), a 44 ppt event on-target. The next observation should be in a red (r', R, i', I, z, Y) filter to refine the timing of the transit on target. Felipe Murgas/MuSCAT2 observed a nominal full on 20220324 in rp,zs and detected a late (~8 min) 50.80, 44.34 ppt event using an uncontaminated 10.9 target aperture. Felipe Murgas/ MuSCAT2 observed a full on 20240516 in gp, rp, ip, zs and detected a ~15 min. early ~69.5, 49.7, 50.6, 44.0 ppt event using an uncontaminated 10.9" target aperture. The gp and rp bands suffer from large scatter and are likely inconclusive. [P=2.0685832] Joel Hartman and Gaspar Bakos report an SEB1 false positives from several KPF observations (K = 80.3 km/s). Retired as SEB1."

name: TOI03956.01
comments: EPHEMERIS ERROR: 12min Observed with MuSCAT2 on Mar. 5, 2022. Here is a report to TFOP: We observed a full transit of TOI-3956.01 in g, r, zs bands with MuSCAT2 on Mar. 5, 2022. With an aperture size of 25 pixels (10.9), which fully contains the brighter neighbor with dT=1.0 mag at 4.55" (TIC 309849816), we detected ~1, 2, and 3 ppt transits in g, r, and zs bands, respectively. The observed depth, which is possibly deeper in redder bands, is consistent with the transit signal observed by TESS (a 7 ppt transit is contaminated by the slightly bluer and ~1 mag brighter neighbor). Note that we tried to separate the neighbor from the target, however, with a decent seeing condition of ~1.2", we could not directly identify which is the source of the transit signal."
comments_sg1: V-shaped; found in faint-star QLP search; Greg Srdoc/Kotizarovci observed a full on 20220124 in ~T and detected an ~on time ~5-7 ppt V-shaped transit using 7.2 target aperture contaminated by T2 TIC 309849816 (T = 12.038, ΔT = -1.04, r = 4.08"). Ferran Grau/CALOU-0m4 analyzed a full on 20220125 in R and probably ruled out the 7 ppt event, and detected an ~on-time ~1.5 ppt event using a 8.9" target aperture that is contaminated with a ΔT = -1.0 neighbor 4.1" W. Felipe Murgas/MuSCAT2 observed a nominal full on 20220305 in gp,rp,zs and detected a ~6 min (0.5σ) early 1.67, 2.44, 3.52 ppt (contaminated depth) event using a 10.9" target aperture that is contaminated with a dT=-1.04 neighbor r = 4.08" (TIC 309849816). Chromaticity on target or due to the 4" neighbor?? No more observations needed, except for a focused high precision (<1.0 ppt/5 min) full transit in a blue (U, u', B, g') and/or very red (zp, zs, Y) filter to that separate the 4" neighbor and determine the source of the event and check chromaticity. Multi-band observations preferred."

name: TOI05655.01
comments: RV interest by Ilaria
comments_sg1: found in faint-star QLP search; ChrisS/ CristilynW/ LCO-McD-1m0 analyzed a nominal full on 20240519 in ip and maybe detected a ~40-50 min early (3.5σ) early ~1.3 ppt egress using an uncontaminated 3.9 target aperture and cleared the neighbors for NEBs over the window. There is a hit to the sky at nominal ingress that makes the interpretation of the possible ingress timing/duration challenging. Let's try again at the nominal ephemeris. The next observation should be a high precision (<1.0 ppt/10 min) full transit in a red (r', R, i', I, z, Y) filter to attempt a firm detection of the event on target. Multi-band or blue (U, u', B, g') filter observations even better for a simultaneous chromaticity check."

name: TOI02018.01
comments: Young planet. Jaume's project. Observed on 15/06. Report: The observations were affected by dome vignetting, these points were removed from the transit fit. We detect an egress on target, the transit occurs earlier than the prediction. If the detection is correct the transit happened 3.9 hours earlier than the prediction (Tc measured = 9746.4255418 ± 0.001914, Tc pred = 9746.5884). Re-observe once more, check ephemeris have been updated (old Period: 7,4371152304643) Observed on 17 March. Report: We detect an ingress on target. The target star was saturated a little bit in i- and zs-bands (9 % and 15%), so next time you may try to defocus during the observations if this happens.
comments_sg1: [P=7.4371150] potential L1 candidate; possible multi as other transit like signals in LC; Greg Srdoc observed a deep full (e-264/i+225 min; -3.8/+3.2σ) on UT2021.02.24 in T and cleared the field of NEBs. Bob Massey analyzed a scheduled full (e-207/i+231 min;-2.4/+2,6 σ) on 20210517 in 1m0 zs. Inconclusive due to bad guiding. Rick Schwarz/LCO-CTIO-1m0 analyzed a nominal full on 20220326 in zs and apparently detected an 76 min (0.5σ) late ~3.5 ppt (3x too deep) transit in an uncontaminated 5.9 target aperture. The depth seems conclusively different than SPOC. The depth is not majorly affected by comp ensemble or detrending, but given the large ephemeris uncertainty, I'm not clear how to interpret. We'll get an ephemeris update from s51 TICAs (ending May 18th). Michael Zhang/LCO-Teid-1m0 observed an egress on 20220410 in ip and apparently detected a ~220 min (~1.4σ) early ~1.2 ppt egress using an uncontaminated 9.7" target aperture. [P=7.43552] Rick Schwarz/LCO-McD-1m0 analyzed an ingress on 20220417 in zs and with double-detrending possibly detected a 56 min (14σ) late ~1.1 ppt ingress in an uncontaminated 5.1" target aperture. However, the event is not obvious in the undetrended light curve, and the would-be pre-baseline is not flat. With airmass detrending, the lightcuve is consistent with flat, except for the 30 min divot at predicted ingress. After reviewing the previously reported detection's undetrended light curve, I'm not convinced this timing is correct. Return to original [P=7.437115] Felipe Murgas/MuSCAT2 observed a defocused egress on 20220615 in gp,rp,ip,zs and detected a ~230 min (0.8σ) early 1.02, 1.45, 0.74, 1.46 ppt event using an uncontaminated 10.87" target aperture. [P=7.4355787] Felipe Murgas/MuSCAT2 observed a defocused egress on 20230317 in gp,rp,ip,zs and detected an on-time 1.48, 2.31, 1.72, 1.33 ppt egress using an uncontaminated 10.87" target aperture. No more SG1 observations needed."

name: TOI01293.01

Felipe and the MuSCAT2 team observed an odd (n=167) full on 20200426 in gp,rp,ip,zs and possible detected a 1 ppt chromatic transit (0 pppt in gp and rp). LCO 1m zs: 2 ppt (n=166) (on time) LCO 1m zs: 1 ppt (n=173) (20 min early) MuSCAT2 ip,zs: 1 ppt (n=167) (~20 min late) LCO 1m gp, rp: ~0.5 ppt. MuSCAT2 gp, rp: ~ 0 ppt Possible BEB. On hold awaiting RVs for final call on disposition."

name: TOI01805.01
comments_sg1: [P=24.0730680] Possibly instrument noise; Jacob Kamler analyzed an egress -45/+75 min (-1/+1.5 sigma) on 20200504 in 0m4 ip and maybe detected a roughly 15 min late egress in a 4.6'' aperture. However the weather hit at about the same time makes a firm interpretation difficult. Update to SPOC s14-s55. [P=24.0512956] ChrisS/ CristilynW/ LCO-McD-1m0 analyzed an egress+90% on 20240416 in zs and possibly detected a ~46 min early (3.5σ) early ~1 ppt egress using an uncontaminated 4.3 target aperture. AIJ s76 TICA analysis shows an event that is consistent with 0-10 min early. Let's try again to see if we can get better precision. Howie Relles/ CristilynW/ analyzed a weather-affected full on 20240510 in zs and probably detected an ~on-time ~1.3 ppt event using an uncontaminated 4.7" target aperture. The next observation should be a high precision (<1.0 ppt/10 min) full transit in a red (r', R, i', I, z, Y) filter to attempt a firm detection of the event on target. Multi-band or blue (U, u', B, g') filter observations even better for a simultaneous chromaticity check."

comments: EPHEMERIS ERROR: 17 min Teq=683K Confirm event on target with high precision (<~1.5ppt/min) and/or check field for NEBs within 2.5' using a red (rp, R, ip, I, z) filter. Observed on 20/04. Report: The i-band CCD had connection issues and could not be used. The data points at the end of the time series were affected by a large drift in X-axis, these points were not included in the transit fit. The pipeline does not find a strong evidence for a transit event, however the 10 min. bin curve shows a late transit event on target. This is a tentative transit detection. We can ruled out 1 nearby star as an EB. Not100% sure of the transit detection, the fit is quite sensitive by the removal of the part of the light curve when the x-axis drift happend (see covariate plots).

name: K2-33b
comments: Narita. A young planet around a pre-main sequence star. super-Neptune. Detecting Rayleigh slope may constrain planet density. As many as opportunities are desired. Also proposed by: J. de Leon and J. Livingston for atmospheres of planets in a young cluster We propose to observe a relatively unexplored population planets in young clusters. Due to their youth, these planets are experiencing high levels of UV irradiation which is the main factor of haze formation. Depending on their age, some younger planets are still undergoing contraction and hence their atmosphere if it exists are relatively puffy than current estimates. Investigating the wavelength-dependent radius variation can provide an insight to the existence such atmosphere and haze from the Rayleigh slope. 16/7/19 To be analyzed properly. No transit in the quicklook, ephemeris are lost

[P=1.22395] Felipe and the MuSCAT2 team observed a full on 20210507 in gp,rp,ip,zs and marginally detected a few min late ~1 ppt event on target. Greg Srdoc and Karen Collins analyzed a full on 20210516 from LCO-M3 in gp, rp, ip, zs and detected a chromatic 0, 0.5, 1.5, 2.5 event in a 5.4" target aperture that is contaminated with a speckle ΔI = 3.3 neighbor 0.83" SE. Eric Jensen conducted a joint MCMC analysis and finds that the amount of extra flux needed increases strongly toward the blue, with as much as 10x or more contaminating flux is needed in gp as in zs. Looking back at the two previously reported MuSACT2 achromatic transits, I think they are both consistent with the MuSCAT3 chromatic results and show a deeper event in red than in blue. Eric's analysis suggests the 0.8" companion hosts the transits since the nominal TOI is a relatively hot star, which would likely dilute the transit more in the blue if the event is on the ΔI = 3.3 fainter neighbor (assuming it is a cooler star). Assuming T_mag ~ I_mag, the depth in the neighbor would be ~35 ppt. Without a more full characterization of the neighbor, it's not clear if it is an EB or possibly hosts a transiting planet. As Eric also mentions, maybe the nominal TOI star could still host the eclipse if the primary is itself an unresolved binary. If that’s the case, we might again have the issue of whether the eclipse would have to be too deep (after accounting for dilution) to be planetary. Retired as APC/BEB."

comments_sg1: low SNR; possible offset towards another star to the north; Felipe and the MuSCAT2 team observed a full on 20210131 in gp,rp,ip,zs and did not see any obvious NEBs. Bob Massey/LCO-TEID-1m0 analyzed a deep full(e-208/i+204;-3.8/+3.8σ) on 20220630 in ip and cleared NEBs in 33 out of 35 neighbors over the window. T3 that is suggested as a possible source by SPOC centroids does not show a clear event (although it would be only a couple of ppt deep). T4 shows an apparent too-short V-shaped event, but it could be caused by contamination from the very close brighter T5 since it is correlated with a hit to FWHM. Looks very SV like in shape in DVRs; found at 2x alerted period in spoc-s14-s55 and 4x alerted period in spoc-s14-s60. Retired as FA.

name: TOI01131.01
comments: TFOP 19/3/2020 Report: The transit is too shallow to be detected with the current photometric precision, however we can ruled out nearby EBs.
comments_sg1: The star 6 E is a 2mass artifact. DLatham: The two FIES velocities show a significant shift that is out of phase with the photometric ephemeris. The transit duration is very short, and the target star is somewhat evolved, which casts some doubt on whether it hosts a planet with a half day period. Pablo Lewin observe a deep full on 20191020 in I. Inconclusive. Kim McLeod and team observed a full and cleared T3, T4, T7, T8, T11, T12, T16, T18, T19, T20, T21. Felipe and the MuSCAT2 team observed a full on 20200317 in gp,rp,ip,zs and didn't see any obvious NEBs in the brighter 7 nearby stars. We observed a full transit and saturated the target to check for NEBs. A few of the nearby stars saturated (T5, T12, T16, and T2 from residual T1) and were not cleared. All other stars look OK. The next observation should use deep exposures to capture an ingress and/or egress in a red (r', R, i', I, z) filter to conclusively search the nearby field for NEBs."

name: TOI01269.01
comments: Ephemeris error: ~10 min (ephemeris from SPOC S14-19) TFOP 8/5/2020 Report: The transit is detected on target and with a transit depth larger than the predicted. There is some possible chromaticity (shallower depth in zs) but considering the uncertainties this is not statistical significant. SPOC S15 PC: Possible L1 candidate; Confirm event on target with high precision (<~1.5ppt/min) and/or check field for NEBs within 2.5' using a red (rp, R, ip, I, z) filter. FOV: https://drive.google.com/open?id=11U-1fYvdIxumXI5OwA7JnoXNukFo6lNx
comments_sg1: [P=4.2529473] Possible L1 candidate; Felipe and the MuSCAT2 team observed a full on 20200501 in gp,rp,ip,zs and apparently detected 3 ppt events (way too deep) events iin 14 target apertures. Greg Srdoc/LCO-McD-1m0 analyzed a full on 20220516 in rp and probably detected a ~0-17 min (1.5σ) late ~1 ppt event using uncontaminated 8.6" target aperture. The skies we too rough to be fully reliable at the 0.7 ppt level. Rick Schwarz/KarenC/LCO-Teid-1m0 analyzed a full on 20220528 in ip and detected a 17 min (1.3σ) late ~1.7 ppt transit in an uncontaminated 5.5" target aperture. In an independent reduction, the event is decidedly deeper than 0.7 ppt using a variety of comp ensembles and detrending pars. The nearest .02 event is over a day later, so the two transits are apparently not overlapping. [P=4.2533212] Rick Schwarz/ KarenC/ LCO-Teid-1m0 analyzed a full on 20220614 in ip and possibly detected a 27 min (2.0σ) late ~0.8 ppt transit in an uncontaminated 6.6" target aperture. An alternate reduction shows a possible 60 min late 1.8 ppt ingress+90%, which has a similar depth to events seen in two other full transits. AIJ analysis of TESS data shows a depth consistent with QLP and SPOC (0.7 ppt). While this deeper event is only seen with specific detrending, the other two 1.8 ppt full transits appeared to be firm depths. Looking at a few individual transits in the TESS data to see if the depth could be watered down due to large TTVs (which we seem to be seeing from the ground
depth_sg1: +/- ~60 minutes), the TESS depth appears to still be 0.7 ppt. The field is not crowded. Howie Relles/ LCO-TEID-1m0 analyzed a full on 20230603 in ip and detected a ~33 min. early ~1.8 ppt event in an uncontaminated 4.7" target aperture.The timing is consistent with QLP s52, which is shown in the alternate plot. The depth has been 2x SPOC twice. Rick Schwarz/ KarenC/ LCO-Teid-1m0 analyzed a full on 20240416 in ip and likely detected a 0-15 min early ~0.7 ppt transit using an uncontaminated 6.6" target aperture. Rick Schwarz/ LCO-Teid-1m0 analyzed a full on 20240503 in ip and detected a ~7 min (1.8σ) early ~0.9 ppt event using an uncontaminated 5.5" target aperture. No more observations needed, except for extreme precision (<0.4 ppt/10 min) full transits to monitor the timing variations of the very shallow event on target in a red (r', R, i', I, z, Y) filter."

The target maybe too shallow for MuSCAT2. Retired."

Would be only ~3 ppt deep in the blended aperture. The target could be either star, so set exposure appropriately for the brighter star. Roberto Zambelli/LCO-TEID-0.4m analyzed a full on 20230429 in ip and detected a ~on-time 4ppt V-shaped event (or a ~2ppt flat-bottomed event) in a contaminated 6.2" target aperture. T2 (TIC 115270630 T = 12.1 ΔT = -0.9 r = 3.8”). Felipe Murgas/ MuSCAT2 observed a nominal ingress on 20230428 in gp,rp,ip,zs and detected a ~5 min. early 2.32, 3.96, 3.45, 0.70 ppt event using a contaminated 10.9" target aperture on the T2. Ferran Grau/ CALOU-0m4 analyzed a gapped-full on 20240419 in rp and detected a ~6 min early ~4.2 ppt blended event using a 6.9" aperture that contains the flux from T1 and T2 (dT=-0.9, r=3.8”). AdamP+KrzysztofB/SUTO-Otivar-0m3 observed a full on 2025-06-30 in R and couldn't extract a lightcurve for T1, but using 1.9” aperture and didn't see an obvious event on T2. The next observation should be a focused (must separate 4” neighbor) full transit in a red (r', R, i', I, z) filter to determine the source of the event."

name: TOI01753.01
comments: EPHEMERIS ERROR: 18 min We can take a second transit to refine the 3x larger than expected detection Submitted to TFOP on 25/5/2020 Report: We detect a deep achromatic transit event on target. Tc: 2458991.4244943 ± 0.0031721 T14: 0.12966 ± 0.00631 days Submitted to TFOP on 29/6/2020 Report: Tc: 2459384.5220231 ± 0.0045748 T14: 0.15663 ± 0.0081 days We had to remove all the points after T = 2459384.60 BJD due to dome vignetting. We have a tentative ingress detection on target, although the detection is quite sensitive to the removal of a few outlier points at the beginning of the observations. This is probably due to the short pre-ingress data available. The data may be useful to improve the target ephemeris.
comments_sg1: Eric Girardin observed a full (Tc-4/+5 sigma) on 20200521 with no filter and cleared the field. Felipe and the MuSCAT2 team observed an egress on 20200521 in gp,rp,ip,zs and apparently detected a 30 min (1.1 sigma) early 2.3 ppt (3x too deep) egress in an uncontaminated 6 target aperture. Felipe/MuSCAT2 observed a full on 20210618 in gp,rp,ip,zs and saw a hint of a 2σ late ingress on target. Bob Massey/LCO-Teid-1m0 analyzed a defocused, weather affected/gapped, full on 20220507 in ip and say a hint (if large systematics assumed in undetrended light curve) of a 23 min (1.2σ) late 0.6 ppt event using an uncontaminated 5.8" target aperture. There is also a possible 150 min (15σ), 50% too short, ~2 ppt event in the undetrend light curve (this is only mentioned because the potential 2-3 ppt event was seen previously by MuSCAT2). The QLP and SPOC reports appear to rule out and odd/even timing offset or depth difference, so I suspect the deeper events are ground-based systematics. Rick Schwarz/ CristilynW/ LCO-McD-1m0 analyzed a full on 20240605 in ip and detected an ~on-time ~1.0 ppt event using an uncontaminated 5.5" target aperture. No more observations needed."

name: TOI02109.01
comments: EPHEMERIS ERROR: 3 min We want observations during May 2021 for an ongoing paper !! Observations on 6 June 20201 bad due to weather First observation bad due to weather, need a second one Event confirmed on target already. Observed defocussed and go for high precision photometry Submitted to TFOP 22/9/2020 Report: Tc: 2459095.5816143 ± 0.0006803 T14: 0.16944 ± 0.00163 days We detect a clear achromatic transit on the target. Unfortunately the quality of raw photometry is affected by a misbehaving dome leading to varying levels of vignetting, but the relative photometry is still good enough for a good transit fit. The vignetting causes systematics that lead to the r-band radius ratio to be smaller than what at other passbands, but otherwise the radius ratio is securely achromatic.
comments_sg1: [P=0.67249] large; secondary; phantom star (2mass artifact) 392476079 close by; The two TRES observations yield a velocity offset of 2.9 km/s that is consistent with the photometric ephemeris and may be significant despite the rapid rotation. The implied mass of the companion would be more than 5 Jupiters. John Kielkopf observed a full on 20200731 in ULMT rp and detected an on-time on-target ~6 ppt transit in an uncontaminated 4.3 aperture. Pablo Lewin observed a full on 20200731 in alternating 0m4 gp,ip and detected a ~7 ppt transit on target in gp, but the ip light curve is inconsistent with TESS and the simultaneous nice observation in rp by JohnK with ULMT. I'm chalking up the inconsistent ip light curve to usual LCO 0m4 systematics. Pablo Lewin observed a full on 20200802 in MLO Ic and detected a roughly 5 ppt on-time transit.Allyson Bieryla observed a ingress (cut short by weather) on 20200731 with in zp and detected an on-time 7-8 ppt ingress in a 7.4" aperture. Peyton Brown analyzed a full on 20200824 in B, zs and detected on-time ~6 ppt transits in both filters. Felipe and the MuSCAT2 team observed a full on 20200804 in gp,rp,ip,zs and detected an ~on-time achromatic ~5.5 ppt transit in gp,ip,zs in a 14" aperture. Rick Schwarz analyzed a full on 20210407 in 1m0 ip and detected an ~10 early ~7 ppt transit on target. EricG observed a full on 20210407 in GpD ip and detected a ~10 min early ~6 ppt event on target. Giuseppe Marino observed a full on 20210407 in R and detected a 10 min early transit on target. [P=0.67247] Rick Schwarz analyzed fulls on 20210524 in MuSCAT3 gp-rp-ip-zs and detected 6 min longer ~3 min late 6.6 ppt transits on target. Rick Schwarz analyzed fulls on 20210626 in MuSCAT3 gp-rp-ip-zs and detected ~on-time ~ 6.5 ppt transits in all four bands in uncontaminated 5.3" target apertures. Rick Schwarz/LCO-HAL-2m0M analyzed defocused fulls on 20220407 in gp-rp-ip-zs and detected 7 min (0.9) late 115 min duration (7 min [0.9σ] longer) ~6.5 ppt transits in all four bands in uncontaminated 7.8" target apertures. K. Barkaoui/LCO-CTIO-1m0 analyzed a full on 20220414 in zs and detected an ~on-time ~7 ppt event using an uncontaminated 7" target aperture. The low impact model appears to be too short, but a higher impact model fit is consistent with the current duration. AdamP/SUTO-Pyskowice-0m3 osberved a full on 20220703 in 0m3 R and detected on-time ~5.5 ppt transit using 7.54" uncontaminated aperture. Rick Schwarz/LCO-HAL-0m4p analyzed a full on 20230705 in ip and detected an ~on-time ~7.5 ppt event [(Rp/R*)²: 7.2 ppt] using an uncontaminated 8.5" target aperture. Chris Stockdale/LCO-McD-0m35 analyzed a near-full (no post-baseline) on 20230722 in ip and detected an ~on-time ~5.4 ppt event using an uncontaminated 5.1" target aperture. Rick Schwarz/LCO-CTIO/TEID-1m0 analyzed a full x2 on 20230801 in zs and detected a ~on-time ~7.5 , 7.7 ppt [(Rp/R*)²: 7.3, 7.4 ppt] event using a uncontaminated 8.6", 4.3" target apertures. No more observations needed at this time."

depth_sg1: -2σ/+1.4σ) on 20250604 in ip. The data are consistent with a ~30 min (0.7σ) early 1.3 pp ingress using uncontaminated 4.7" elliptical aperture to better measure the elongated PFS. We can rule out 7 stars as EBs with deep transits during the observed window.

[P=3.1434423] Felipe Murgas/MuSCAT2 observed a defocused full on 20240331 in gp,rp,ip,zs and cleared 7 out of 19 neighbors over the window, did not see an obvious NEB. T2 could not be cleared. comments_sg1: [P=3.1291319] low SNR; ambiguous difference image but no nearby ephemeris matches; John Kielkopf/ ULMT observed a nominal ful on UTC 20240426 in rp and maybe detected a ~65 min early ~1 ppt egress using an uncontaminated 4.4 target aperture. The next TESS observation is June 2024 and hopefully we can get an updated ephemeris. For now, let's try this possible early event. [P=3.1289281] John Kielkopf/ ULMT observed a full on 20240612 in ip and cleared 24/27 NEBcheck stars (but not T2, T3, T14). There is a hint of a 25 min (1.8σ) late ~1.7 ppt (3x QLP) event on-target, but this timing is inconsistent with the previous hint at a 71 minute early event. Using SPOC FFI sectors 25 & 52, and TICA FFI sector 79, AIJ does not find this low SNR 680 ppm QLP signal on target, or any indication of the event off target. QLP did not detect the signal in the single sector 25. AndrewV confirms that his pipeline also does not see the siignal. Return to nominal period [P=3.1291319] Jerome de Leon/ MuSCAT2 team/ observed a full on 20240605 in gp, rp, ip, zs and probably ruled out the ~0.7 ppt event using an uncontaminated 10.9" target aperture. No more SG1 observations. Retired as FA."

name: TOI01832.01
comments: EPHEMERIS ERROR: 26 min Report to TFOP by Akihiko: We observed TOI-1832.01 from +0 to +3.2 hours from the predicted transit time on 2021 May 27 in g, r, and i bands with MuSCAT2. Although the data are sparse due to cloudy weather, at least we can rule out on-time 9 ppt transit on target. We also did not observe any apparent NEB, which however is inconclusive due to the poor weather. Observed on 26 of March. Report: We detect a transit event with an aperture that is contaminated by the flux of Star ID 3. We tried to fit the transit using smaller apertures to check which star has the transit but we did not get good results, the stars are too close.

name: TOI04600.02
comments: Extremely long period planet candidate.
comments_sg1: Update to Ismeal Mireles ephermeris [P=482.8191, T0=2751.6008] Ingress/Egress duration should be ~80 minutes. Nikita Chazov/ Kourovka Observatory 0.4m (x2) observed a gapped ingress+50% on 20231016 in alternating V, R and detected an ~30 min early 14 ppt [(Rp/R*)²= 12, 11 ppt] event using an uncontaminated 5.5 target aperture. Nicholas Dowling/ Khushi Marlecha/ WST observed a gapped egress+10% on 20231016 in r',i',J and with double-detrending maybe detected a ~80 min early ~16, 11, 12 ppt egress using uncontaminated 2.4", 2.4", 2.25" target aperture. An alternative analysis using single detrending in ip, J shows an possible ~on-time 8.2, 13.9 ppt egress while rp band seems to favor the early "egress-looking" wiggle. The field is spase so there may have been a challenge choosing good comp stars. Given the low uncertiantly in the ephemeris and the simultaneous observation (ingress+50%) by Nikita Chazov/ Kourovka Observatory that found an ~on-time ~14 ppt event in alternating V,R, It seems most likely that the event is ~on-time and probably the predicted duration. Francis Wilkin/ iTel-T21-0m43 analyzed a weather-affected egress+8% on 20231016 in R and with double-detrending maybe detected an ~on-time 6.6 ppt event target using an uncontaminated 5.2" aperture. It is not ideal that the ~on-time, shallower than expected, event is only modelled when all parameters are locked for duration/timing and two detrending parameters are used, otherwise it is flat. Likely inconclusive. Bob Massey/ Villa39 observed an egress+13% on 20231017 in I and probably detected an ~on-time ~9 ppt egress using an uncontaminated 9.5" target aperture. David Baker/ AdamsObs observed an egress+24% on 20231016 in Ic and probably detected an ~on-time 6.6 ppt [(Rp/R*)²=9.6 ppt] egress using an uncontaminated 4.2" target aperture. Bob Aloisi/ Lizhou Sha/ Jenna Karcheski/ Alyssa Jankowski/ BBO/ observed an egress+20% on 20231016 in a Luminance (UV/IR) filter and probably detected an ~on-time ~8.6 ppt egress using an uncontaminated 10" target aperture. Arthur Sweeney/ SOFAR-0m.4 observed an egress+15% on 2023.10.17 in gp and possibly detected a ~25 min late ~10.6 ppt egress using an uncontaminated 7.5" target aperture. Continue to monitor possible TTVs in the band that should give the best precision with your equipment."

comments: Very long period planet to confirm for Emma/Alberto/Jaume Observed on May 28 2024 Report: The observations were affected by guiding issues at the start and dome vignetting at the end, the affected data were not considered for the transit fit. We do not detect the transit event with significance. There could be a hint of an egress but this could be an spurious detection. We can rule out 2 stars as EBs with deep transits during the observed window. I don't know if the egress is real, this happens after the guiding issues that caused a 'jump' of the FoV (see 'covariates' plot). I tried a fit using this part of the light curve but the final fit had transit too deep in r-band. By using only star ID 2 as reference I also got the same result (I was worried that star ID 1 was too close to the edge of the field).

name: TOI07144.01
comments: Observed on Feb. 18, 2025. Autoguide did not work well, resulting in a large scatter. Let's try again. Observed on 25/05. 2025 Report: The target aperture is contaminated by the flux of Star ID 51 (TIC 400235558, dT = 3.60, r = 4.23). We do not detect the transit event in the observed window (target has TTVs according to SG1). We can rule out 61 stars as EBs with deep transits during the observed window. observed on 22/04 2025 Report: We restricted the target aperture size to avoid flux contamination from the nearby Star ID 54 (dT = 3.6, r = 4.22", SE, TIC 400235558). We do not detect the transit event with significance, but the photometry is likely affected by the nearby star, even if the telescope was focused. We can only rule out 46 stars as EBs with deep transits during the observed window."
comments_sg1: found in faint-star QLP search; Bob Massey/LCO-TEID analyzed a full (+/- > 10σ) on 20250422 in ip and ruled out a 2.5 ppt event over the window using a 3.1 target aperture. The field was cleared of NEBs over the window. There are apparent long super-period TTVs in the TESS data. Apparently the ephemeris has shifted significantly since sector 79. {Expanding T0 from 0.002 to 0.2] Rick Schwarz/ KarenC/ LCO-CTIO-1m0 analyzed a full (e-295/i+129
depth_sg1: >+/-10σ) on 20250426 in ip and ruled out the 2.5 ppt event in T1 and T2 using a 6.6" target aperture that is fully contaminated with the nearby T2 (TIC 400235558: T=16.3 dT=3.6 r=4.2" SW). Additional aperture sizes cleared the field of NEBs. MuSCAT2 Team observed a nominal full on 20250525 in gp, rp, ip, zs and cleared 61 out of 116 neighbors over the window, did not see an obvious NEB. MuSCAT2 Team observed a nominal full on 20250422 in gp, rp, ip, zs and ruled out the event on target and cleared 46 out of 116 neighbors over the window, did not see an obvious NEB. This is the third no-show of the event on and off target over a many σ window, so apparent the TTVs seen in TESS have caused us to lose the ephemeris. No more SG1 observations needed at this time."

name: TOI01610.01
comments: Added on Mar 21, 2020 by Akihiko. Ephemeris error: 8min. Comments on TFOP: Potential L1 candidate; low SNR; Passes TRES Recon. Check field within 2.5' for NEBs using a red (rp, R, ip, I, z) filter. TFOP 4/5/2020 Report: Although the weather condition was not perfect (cirrus passed on and off), we likely detected a 2x deeper transit on the target on time. We also cleared the field except for star ID=3 and ID=4. 28/3/2020 Report: Data is not great, but good. No transit visible in the data. The blend analysis is also inconclusive. I will put the target for one more observation, and if we do not get better data, then it might be too difficult for Muscat2. 30/4: Observation #2 was also useless due to weather. Let's try one more time Submitted to TFOP 18/5/2020 Report: We observed another transit of TOI 1610.01 on May 3rd to confirm or refute the 2x deeper transit we report from the data taken on April 28th. As a result, we do not detect the 2x deeper transit or the original shallow transit. Star ID 3 and 4 are still not completely cleared, but we do not find any sign of dimming for those 2 stars in any band both on April 28th and May 3rd. Enric moves this target to priority 5
comments_sg1: Potential L1 candidate; (S14-19, no longer low SNR; Passes TRES Recon. Felipe and the MuSCAT2 team observed a gapped even (n=112) full on 20200428 in gp,rp,ip,zs and apparently detected a 2 min late ~0.9 ppt (2x too deep) transit in an uncontaminated 6.1 target aperture. Felipe and the MuSCAT2 team observed an even (n=114) full on 20200503 in gp,rp,ip,zs and did not detect the >2x too deep event detected in the previous MuSCAT2 observation. 6 of 32 neighbors are cleared. David Baker/AdamsObs observed a deep full on 20220707 in Ic and cleared NEBs in 19 out of 27 neighbors over the window. David Baker/AdamsObs observed a deep full on 20220707 in Ic and cleared NEBs in 19 out of 27 neighbors over the window. Combined with the SPOC s14-s50 centroids, the field is cleared. Bob Massey/ KarenC/ /LCO-Teid-1m0 analyzed a defocused full on 20220702 in zs and measured a tentative on-time 0.4 ppt event using an uncontaminated 9.3" target aperture. On hold awaiting validation or confirmation interest. No more SG1 observations needed at this time."

name: TOI02121.01
comments: False positive. Retired by SG1
comments_sg1: V-shaped and deep transit.; Fabio SALVAGGIO & Giuseppe MARINO observed a full on 20200807 in clear and detected an on-time 14 ppt V-shaped transit in a 7.1 uncontaminated target aperture. Pablo Lewin observed a full on 20200810 in MLO Ic and detected a 7 min late 20 ppt V-shaped transit in an 5.9" aperture that is not contaminated with any Gaia stars bright enough to cause the event. Hannu and Judith report a ~5 min odd/even timing shift, which is a little less convincing in the individual transits, but is significant in the combined odd/even timing (bottom-left plot in TIC_88996391_01.png). The transit is V-shaped and the RUWE is large too. We now have 5 TRES observations. The first three are grossly underexposed and are useless. The two recent observations yield an offset of 634 m/s that is in phase with the photometric ephemeris and is probably significant. John Kielkopf observed a full on 20210513 un ULMT rp and detected a ~30 min late ~7 ppt V-shaped event on target. Retired as APC/BEB."
rv_interested: Outer stellar companion P=248d, Jason interested

name: TOI05776.01
comments_sg1: found in faint-star QLP search; Avraham, Aviad, Tsevi, and Shay report from 66 Gaia data points (12 in-transit), a possible 5 ppt event on-target. Allyson Bieryla/KeplerCam observed an egress on 20220906 in ip and detected an on-time 6 ppt egress using an uncontaminated 5.4 target aperture. Howie Relles/LCO-TEID-1m0 analyzed an ingress+95% on 20230609 in gp and detected an ~on-time ~7 ppt ingress in an uncontaminated 7.4" target aperture. No more observations needed, except for **full-only** multi-band or alternating filter high precision (<1.0 ppt/10 min) data in at least blue (U, u', B, g') *and* red (i', I, z, Y) filters to refine the check on chromaticity."

comments_sg1: [P=16.216178] Felipe and the MuSCAT2 team observed a full on 20200425 in gp,rp,ip,zs and did not detect an obvious NEB in the brightest 11 of 28 Gaia NEBcheck stars within 2.5'. Ana Clara Segal observed a full (e-269/i+294 min; -14.2/+15.5 sigma) on 20210520 in 1m0 ip and detected an apparent 35 min (1.8σ) early ~2.5 ppt V-shaped event in an uncontaminated 5.7 target aperture. No obvious strong chromaticity, but guiding was not good and depths depend on comp ensemble and detrending selected. Rick Schwarz analyzed fulls on 20210809 in MuSCAT3 gp-rp-ip-zs and detected ~30 min (1.4σ) early ~ 2.0 ppt transits in uncontaminated 6.6" target apertures. A fairly large in-transit systematic is correlated with Y-centroid in most bands due to tracking/guiding problems. CristilynW/ LCO-Teid-1m0 analyzed a full on UT2023.08.08 in alternating gp, ip and detected a ~on-time, V-shaped, ~30 min longer, ~3.6 ppt [(Rp/R*)²: 4.1, 4.6 ppt] (2x SPOC) event using uncontaminated 4.7" target apertures. Howie Relles/ CristilynW/ LCO-TEID-1m0/ analyzed a full on 20240611 in ip and detected a ~48 min early, ~30 min longer duration, ~2.9 ppt [(Rp/R*)²: 3.8 ppt] event using an uncontaminated 5.1" target aperture. comments_sg1: [P=18.0591050] Originally STPC S14-S19 finds period. Has a dT=3 neighbor 2 NW. PereG observed a full on 20200403 in OAA Ic and possibly detected an on time event in the 16" target aperture, but it is contaminated with a dT=3 neighbor 2" NW (T2). Felipe and the MuSCAT2 team observed a full on 20200420 in gp,rp,ip,zs and clear the brightest 9 of 25 neighbors, and found no clear evidence for the transit on target. There is a hint of a 40 min (~2-sigma) late ingress in ip and zs. Tianjun observed a short noisy egress segment on 20210330 in 1m0 zs. Inconclusive.Ephemeris update from spoc-s14-s50-b0A-PC. [P=18.05747+/-0.00006] ChrisS/ CristilynW/ LCO-Teid-1m0 analyzed a full on 20240615 in zs and detected a ~23 min (2σ) late ~1.1 ppt event using a contaminated 4.3" target aperture (T2: TIC 1400701473: T=13.8, dT=3.1, r=1.7"). [P = 18.05763] The next observation should be a focused high precision (<1.0 ppt/10 min) full transit in a red (r', R, i', I, z) filter using at aperture that attempts to exclude most of the flux from the 1.7" neighbor to determine the source of the event at the revised ephemeris (will be challenging from the ground)."

name: TOI02174.01
comments: EPHEMERIS ERROR: 1h 11min Observations on 19 May2021 bad due to weather. Let's go for a second try Observed on 27/02. Report: The i-band CCD had connection issues and could not be used. We had guiding problems during the observations. We do not detect the transit event on target during the observed window. We also do not detect any deep transit event on the stars nearby during the observed window. Let's go for a third try Observed on May 23 2023 Report: The observations were made with the telescope defocused to avoid saturation from the target star (V = 10.8 mag). The target aperture is contaminated with the flux from Star ID 6 (TIC 1508598052, r = 3.8). We report a tentative transit detection in the 10 min. binned light curve, the transit seems to be achromatic although it is shallow. We cannot confirm in which star the transit occurs."

name: TOI01250.01
comments: EPHEMERIS ERROR: 16 min Submitted to TFOP 18/3/2020 Report: We detect a deep colour-dependent event on TIC 233059601 (star 7) with the event centre within 1 sigma of the expected transit centre. Tc: 2458915.7482229 ± 0.0019483 T14: 0.05828 ± 0.00762 days
comments_sg1: Nearby bright star; Pablo Lewin observed a full on 20191012 in I using deep exposures and cleared all stars except the 14 T2, 21" T3, and 43" T4. Check field within 2.5' for NEBs using a red (rp, R, ip, I, z) filter. Felipe and the MuSCAT2 team observed a full on 20200306 in gp,rp,ip,zs and apparently detected a deep event on the TIC 233059601 (star aperture 7) 15" NNW. However, I don't see the event in the raw light curve for star 7, and in fact it appears to not have enough precision to detect the reported event. The SPOC multi-Sector results do not report a detection of this TOI. The S14-S21 FFIS ruled out the event in a standard 3-pixel aperture. Even S14 rules out the event, although there is some variability in a star in the 3 pixel aperture. Retired as False Alarm (FA)."

name: TOI05769.01
comments_sg1: [P=1.7548863] found in faint-star QLP search; John Kielkopf/ULMT observed a nominal full on 20230702 in rp and did not see an obvious 2.6 ppt event in a 5 target aperture that includes the 2.1" neighbor. Fainter NEBcheck stars are not cleared. Allyson Bieryla/KeplerCam observed a egress+40% on 20230618 in ip and maybe detected a ~16 min late 3.3 ppt event using a contaminated (T2:TIC 1684935059 T=16.9, ΔT=5.3, r=2.1") 3.4" target aperture but the signal is flat with airmass detrending. The field was cleared out to 2', with the exception of T2. John Kielkopf/ULMT observed a full on 20250504 in gp and ruled out the 2.6 ppt event with a contaminated 4.8" radius target aperture (T2, ΔT = 5.3, r = 2.1"). The field was cleared except T2 (2 arcsec | dmag 0.078) and T3 (9 arcsec | dmag 4.5), but apparently T2 is ruled out as the source since the T1 aperture included its flux. SPOC s80 centroids favor an event in T2 or T3, but do not rule out T1. If the event is chromatic, it could still be in T1 or T2. The event is V-shaped in SPOC s80. AIJ s40+s53+s80 shows the ephemeris is accurate within ~10 minutes (1σ). [P=1.7549063, T0=2390.6763]. ChrisS/LCO-McD-CTIO-0m35 analyzed a full on 20250511 in ip and detected a ~on-time, 43 min (4σ) longer 3.8 ppt [(Rp/R*)²: 9.4 ppt] event using an 8" target aperture contaminated with T2 (TIC 1684935059 T=16.9, ΔT=5.3, r=2.1" W), T3 and T4. Using a 4.4" aperture in the McD obs, and opposite aperture detrending, the event occurred in T1, while T2, T3 (ΔT = 6.49 r = 8.89") and T4 (ΔT = 6.71 r = 9.34" W) are clear. Rick Schwarz/ KarenC/ LCO-SAAO-1m0 analyzed a full on 20250604 in gp-ip and apparently ruled out the 2.6 ppt event in both bands using a 7" target aperture that is blended with the nearby T2 (TIC 1684935059: T=16.9 dT=5.3 r=2.1"). Attempts to separate the 2.1" neighbor were inconclusive and T2, T3, and T5 can not be directly ruled out, but a 15" aperture that includes T1, 2, 3, and 4, does not show an obvious event in ip band. SPOC s80 detected the event as V-shaped and favors T2 or T4 5-10" W. There is no QLP s80 report. ChrisS/LCO-CTIO-1m0 analyzed a full on 20250716 in gp, ip and ruled out the 2.6 ppt event on target using a 5.8" target aperture contaminated with T2 (TIC 1684935059: T=16.9 dT=5.3 r=2.1"). SPOC s80 shows this to be a V-shaped event with an eccentric secondary, both with a period of 3 x QLP, or [P= 5.26361, 3482.2361]. Centroids suggest it is nominally off-target on T2 or T3 (both are bright enough to be capable of causing TESS detection). Retirted as EB."

name: MAXIJ1807+132
comments: Only for Sep 25 and 26, 2019. Should be on-focus. Use r-band for guiding and set 30 sec. Set 180 sec (3 min) for g and z bands. Check the field of view attached in the e-mail for this special observation so that specific comparison stars are located within the field of view. Take 3 min x 3 frames for g and z bands, and 30 sec x 18 frames for r band. Scientific explanation is below. MAXI J1807+132 is a transient low-mass X-ray binary, and entered an outburst on Sep. 5th, 2019. Whether the central compact source is a black hole or a neutron star is under debate (e.g., Shidatsu et al. 2017, Jim enez-Ibarra et al. 2019). To answer this, we are planning to extract the time variations of the disk component from soft-X-ray and optical spectra, and identify what the hard-X-ray component comes from. We have a 20-ks time at NuSTAR and 2-ks time at Swift and 1-ks time at NICER on Sep. 26th. The snapshot optical observations at muscat2 at that date help us to derive the information about an accretion disk, i.e., disk radius and temperature distribution and so on.

name: TOI01863.01
comments: EPHEMERIS ERROR: 4 min Submitted to TFOP 21/6/2020. Report:Report: Because we could not isolate a faint (dT=5.6) nearby star (at 7.2” NW) from the target star in the photometry, we fitted for two light curves of the target with different aperture radii, one (rad=23 pix) includes the nearby star while the other (rad=14 pix) does not. As a result, we found a marginal, likely chromatic transit signal only in the larger aperture photometry, which implies that the faint star is an EB that causes the transit signal found by TESS. Period < 1.5 days; Possible hot Jupiter. Confirm event on target with high precision (<~1.5ppt/min) and/or check field for NEBs within 2.5' using a red (rp, R, ip, I, z) filter.
comments_sg1: Likely EB; V-shaped; must be grazing if planet; there is a 300 ppm secondary consistent with a hot Jupiter; there is an ephemeris match to 356919654 but no depth/aperture correlation; need SG1 to check TIC 356919654; CTOI from Ethan Kruse; Felipe and the MuSCAT2 team observed a full on 20200619 in gp,rp,ip,zs and ruled out a 1.9 ppt event on target using a 6.2 aperture that excludes part of the flux from a dT=5.2 neighbor 7.2" NW, and likely detected an on-time chromatic event in a larger 10.1" aperture that includes most of the flux from the 7.2" neighbor. A SPOC DVR has been released since this TOI was released, and it shows a nominal offset ~ 8" NE, but the uncertainty circle marginally includes the target star too. Considering both measurements, retired as NEB."

name: TOI02172.01
comments_sg1: [P=1.7825200] Likely EB; evolved star; asymmetric transit; a/Rs under 2; Update from AIJ-s54. [P=1.78237] Felipe Murgas/ MuSCAT2 observed a defocused full on 20240515 in gp, rp, ip, zs and ruled out a ~1.9 ppt event on target and cleared 10 out of 41 neighbors over the window. AIJ s74 SAFFIs did not show an obvious NEB in the 41 neighboring star apertures. A second review of the TESS data with AIJ does not show an event in any of the sectors, or in the combined sectors. Andrew Vanderburg's pipeline agree that there is no signal and QLP has not reported a signal since s26. Retired as FA.

name: TOI04039.01

period: Confirm event on target and/or check field for NEBs within 2.5' using a red (rp, R, ip, I, z) filter." The transit model produces a better light curve with the largest aperture size available (10.8 arcsec) but there may be some slight flux contamination from Star ID 1 and 3. We can also rule out EBs in the field."

[P=4.730857] Allyson Bieryla observed a near-full on 20210916 in KeplerCam ip and detected an ~on-time ~12 ppt event in an 2.7" target aperture that is contaminated with a 2.4" SW neighbor. No more observations needed, except a focused full transit in a red (r', R, i', I, z) filter to separate and exclude most of the flux from the 2.4" SE neighbor from the T1 aperture."
